questions.question: Explain the concept of agility in software engineering and why it emerged as a response to traditional development approaches.
keyPoints: Agility emphasizes flexibility, iterative development, and customer collaboration
Traditional waterfall models were too rigid for rapidly changing requirements
Agility emerged due to need for faster time-to-market and ability to adapt to changes
Focuses on delivering value to customers through working software

questions.question: List and briefly explain the four core values of the Agile Manifesto.
keyPoints: Individuals and interactions over processes and tools
Working software over comprehensive documentation
Customer collaboration over contract negotiation
Responding to change over following a plan

questions.question: What is the difference between predictive planning and adaptive planning in software development?
keyPoints: Predictive planning defines all requirements upfront and follows a fixed plan
Adaptive planning accepts evolving requirements and continuously revises plans
Agile uses adaptive planning to accommodate changes based on feedback
Predictive approaches struggle with uncertainty while adaptive approaches embrace it

questions.question: Describe the characteristics of an agile team structure.
keyPoints: Cross-functional with diverse skills
Self-organizing without top-down management
Small size (typically 5-9 members)
Colocated for effective communication
Daily collaboration with customer or product owner

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'sustainable pace' in agile methodology and its importance.
keyPoints: Teams should work at a pace that can be maintained indefinitely
Avoids developer burnout and maintains long-term productivity
Reflects the principle of caring about people over rushing
Enables consistent quality delivery over extended periods

questions.question: How does agility help in managing project risks compared to traditional approaches?
keyPoints: Early and continuous delivery provides early visibility of issues
Frequent feedback loops identify problems quickly
Iterative approach breaks large risks into smaller manageable pieces
Adaptive planning allows course correction based on changing conditions

questions.question: Explain the 12 principles behind the Agile Manifesto. Provide a brief explanation of at least 6 principles.
keyPoints: Principle 1: Highest priority is customer satisfaction through early and continuous delivery
Principle 2: Welcome changing requirements even late in development
Principle 3: Deliver working software frequently
Principle 4: Business people and developers work together daily
Principle 5: Build projects around motivated individuals
Principle 6: Face-to-face conversation is most effective communication
Principle 7: Working software is primary measure of progress
Principle 8: Sustainable development pace
Principle 9: Continuous attention to technical excellence
Principle 10: Simplicity - maximize work not done
Principle 11: Self-organizing teams produce best results
Principle 12: Regular reflection and adjustment

questions.question: Discuss how agility addresses the cost of change curve in software development. Compare it with traditional waterfall approach.
keyPoints: Traditional waterfall shows exponential cost increase for changes late in development
Agile keeps cost curve relatively flat through iterative feedback
Changes are incorporated early in the development cycle
Continuous customer collaboration ensures requirements are validated frequently
Working software increments allow early testing and feedback
Iterative approach with short cycles reduces risk and cost of changes
Adaptive planning accepts changes without major rework

questions.question: Explain the relationship between the four values and twelve principles of agile methodology. How do these guide agile practice?
keyPoints: Values define what matters most (the 'why'), principles guide how to achieve it
Values are hierarchical - earlier values take precedence over later
Principle 1 derives from value of working software over documentation
Collaboration value leads to daily customer interaction principle
Responding to change value leads to adaptive planning principle
Principles translate abstract values into actionable guidelines
Values override principles when conflicts arise
Together they form a coherent philosophy for agile software development

questions.question: Describe the concept of iterative and incremental delivery in agile. How does it benefit software development?
keyPoints: Software developed in small time-boxed iterations (typically 1-4 weeks)
Each iteration produces a potentially shippable product increment
Incremental delivery provides early value to customer
Feedback from each increment guides subsequent development
Reduces risk by validating assumptions frequently
Allows course correction based on actual customer needs
Enables continuous improvement of the product
Supports changing requirements throughout development lifecycle
